danger_signals.meaning: Physical danger; platforming challenge or instant death

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark stone chambers with shadow areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright, ornate interior chambers with Persian architectural details
Acrobatic male figure in red/light clothing

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed guards with swords; skeletal/undead variations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dungeon chambers, prison cells, guard posts within palace
Palace interiors; areas of passage and escape

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon environment with stone walls, barred windows, confined spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard patrols block passage; player must solve platforming puzzles to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guards appear in chamber; weapon sprites visible; enemy animations become aggressive
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards pursue player; combat sequences initiate; collision with guards triggers damage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Empty palace corridors; bright lighting; open vertical and horizontal sightlines
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No enemies present; player can move freely; platforming challenges are environmental only

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gaps in floor, platform sequences, moving obstacles, spike traps
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must time jumps and movements; failure results in fall/death; success opens new path

environmental_storytelling.example: Persian palace interior design (arches, columns, tessellated patterns) communicates exotic, enclosed royal setting
Upward chamber layouts suggest escape toward freedom; downward paths suggest descent into danger
Heavily guarded sections indicate strategic chokepoints or valuable areas; sparse sections allow recovery
Brightly lit palace sections feel safer; darker dungeons communicate hostile territory
Spikes and hazards placed at narrow passages communicate designed threat; their visibility teaches danger recognition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks back-and-forth in designated area; sprite animation shows alert posture; no immediate aggression
Sprite animation becomes aggressive; guard moves toward player; weapon raised; collision damages player
Pursues player with erratic movement; faster than humanoid guards; represents uncontrolled threat
Responsive controls; full jump height; fluid platforming animation; no visual impairment
Reduced jump height or slower movement; visual indicator (sprite change or flash) shows damage state

notes: Source provided is a disambiguation page with minimal gameplay mechanics data. T3 tier extraction relies on documented platformer conventions and visual language typical of 1990 Genesis-era action games. The 1989 original Prince of Persia (Apple II) is referenced but the Genesis port (1990) specific visual communication patterns are inferred from platformer paradigm standards of that era. Actual sprite data, color palettes, and confirmed design documentation would be required for higher-confidence mapping.
